answer:
The electric field at (3.0, 3.0) cm is along the direction \(4.0\hat{i} + 3.0\hat{j}\), so the particle must lie on the line through that point with slope \(3/4\).  
At (2.0, 0) cm, the field is purely in the \(+\hat{i}\) direction, so the particle must be directly to the left of that point on the x-axis.  
Solving these conditions gives the particle at \((-2.0, 0)\) cm, so the x-coordinate is \(-2.0\) cm.

\boxed{A}
